author: opus-4.8
decision: Gate the HTTP LOCAL_OPERATOR bypass on deployment posture instead of a per-request loopback peer check.
why: Recon confirmed Next.js has NO custom server and route handlers/middleware cannot read the TCP peer (socket.remoteAddress) — X-Forwarded-For is spoofable/absent without a proxy, so it is not usable for a security decision. The WS plane can peer-check (it owns the socket); the HTTP plane cannot. A posture-based gate is the only sound HTTP-layer mechanism.
implementation: lib/auth-trust.ts localOperatorBypassAllowed(): (1) DAAX_TRUST_LOCAL_OPERATOR explicit both-ways; (2) HOST bind loopback via shared lib/net/loopback.ts -> allow, 0.0.0.0/:: -> deny; (3) NODE_ENV!=='production' -> allow, production -> deny. Ambiguous -> deny (fail-safe). isLoopbackAddress hoisted from server/handlers/ws-auth.ts into lib/net/loopback.ts, shared by both planes (AC5). Change made once in the shared evaluator so requireAuth + the #181 middleware inherit it. Start scripts export HOST=0.0.0.0 on the exposed path.
operator_impact: A proxy-less 0.0.0.0 non-strict container (e.g. galway; both compose files set HOST=0.0.0.0) now returns 401 for the operator-bypass path until DAAX_TRUST_LOCAL_OPERATOR=1 is set OR strict mode (DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH=1) + Pocket ID is used. dev:tailscale likewise. Intended security fix; documented in compose comments, .claude/stack.md, and a one-time startup warning.
validation: Producer Opus 4.8; separate-model Claude Sonnet review PASS on security (host-dev unaffected; exposed->deny; strict/Path-A unaffected; fail-safe; shared loopback dedup; 1593 tests, build ok). Cross-provider (Codex) unavailable per repo memory.
serial_lane: 181 -> 184 (both edit the shared auth evaluator); 181 merged first, so no conflict.

author: opus-4.8
decision: Tighten the NODE_ENV fallback of the LOCAL_OPERATOR bypass from 'NODE_ENV!==production -> allow' to 'NODE_ENV===development -> allow', and make the deny warning state the actual cause.
why: Copilot flagged that the old fallback (NODE_ENV!=='production') treated an UNSET/ambiguous NODE_ENV as non-production and ALLOWED the bypass with no explicit posture signal, contradicting the 'ambiguous -> deny' fail-safe intent. Also warnOperatorBypassBlockedOnce() always blamed exposure even when the deny was an explicit opt-out (DAAX_TRUST_LOCAL_OPERATOR=0) or a production/ambiguous posture.
implementation: lib/auth-trust.ts: extracted evaluateLocalOperatorBypass() returning {allowed:true}|{allowed:false;reason}; branch 3 now allows ONLY when NODE_ENV==='development' (host-dev next dev sets this at runtime) -> unset/test/production all deny fail-safe. localOperatorBypassAllowed() delegates to .allowed (public API unchanged). warnOperatorBypassBlockedOnce(reason) now names the real cause: explicit-opt-out | exposed-beyond-loopback | production-or-ambiguous. evaluateAuthDecision() computes the evaluation once and passes the reason.
operator_impact: Host-dev (next dev, NODE_ENV=development) unchanged -> still allows. Any deployment relying on an unset/test NODE_ENV with no HOST/DAAX_TRUST signal now DENIES (401) instead of silently bypassing. Compose exposed path already denied via HOST=0.0.0.0 (unchanged).
tests: tests/lib/auth.test.ts: NODE_ENV='test' now asserts deny; added 'unset NODE_ENV -> deny' via vi.stubEnv('NODE_ENV',undefined); the 3 main-block operator-bypass tests now set HOST=127.0.0.1 explicitly; tests/middleware.test.ts host-dev cases set HOST=127.0.0.1 in beforeEach. Full suite 1594 passed / 2 skipped; typecheck, lint(0 errors), build all green.
validation: Producer Opus 4.8. Cross-provider (Codex) unavailable per repo memory; separate-model Claude Sonnet validation pending before PR.
serial_lane: Follow-up to the original #184 posture gate; edits the same shared evaluator.
